title: F-1 Student Visa Journey
description: Your complete guide to studying in the United States on an F-1 student visa.
descriptionUr: ایف-۱ سٹوڈنٹ ویزا پر ریاستہائے متحدہ میں تعلیم حاصل کرنے کے لیے آپ کی مکمل رہنمائی۔

steps.name: Research SEVP-Certified Schools
steps.description: You may only pursue F-1 status at a school certified by the Student and Exchange Visitor Program (SEVP). Confirm that your target schools are SEVP-certified before applying.
actions: Search the SEVP School Search at studyinthestates.dhs.gov to verify a school is SEVP-certified
Research programs, admission requirements, deadlines, and tuition at each school
Check English-language proficiency requirements (e.g., IELTS, TOEFL) for your target programs
Review scholarship and funding opportunities (school-specific, HEC, Fulbright, etc.)
Identify the International Student Office (DSO) contact at each school you plan to apply to
steps.output: Target schools confirmed as SEVP-certified; application list finalized

steps.name: Apply to SEVP-Certified School(s)
steps.description: Submit applications to your chosen schools and provide all required documents. Each school sets its own admission requirements and deadlines.
actions: Complete each school's online application form by the stated deadline
Submit required academic transcripts (SSC, HSSC, bachelor's degree, etc.) with official translations if required
Submit standardized test scores (SAT, GRE, GMAT, as applicable)
Submit English proficiency test scores (TOEFL, IELTS, Duolingo, as accepted)
Provide letters of recommendation, personal statement, and other required materials
Pay application fees as required by each institution
Monitor application portals for admission decisions
steps.output: All school applications submitted; confirmation receipts saved

steps.name: Receive Admission Offer & Accept
steps.description: Once accepted, review the offer carefully, confirm financial requirements, and formally accept the offer. The school cannot issue an I-20 until you are admitted and have accepted.
actions: Review admission letter: program, start date, tuition, and any conditions
Confirm you can demonstrate sufficient financial support for the full program (school will require proof for I-20)
Formally accept the offer by the deadline stated in the admission letter
Pay any required enrollment or deposit fee
Contact the International Student Office to begin the I-20 request process
steps.output: Admission accepted; enrollment deposit paid; I-20 request process initiated

steps.name: Submit Financial Documentation to DSO
steps.description: Your Designated School Official (DSO) requires proof of financial support to issue your Form I-20. Acceptable documentation varies by school but typically includes bank statements, sponsor letters, or scholarship award letters.
actions: Request a financial documentation checklist from the school's International Student Office
Gather bank statements (personal, family, or sponsor) covering at least one academic year's costs
Obtain a financial guarantee or sponsorship letter if applicable (signed by sponsor)
If you have a scholarship or funding award, provide the official award letter
Submit all financial documents to the DSO by their stated deadline
Ensure documents are in English or accompanied by certified translations
steps.output: Financial documentation submitted to and accepted by the DSO

steps.name: Receive Form I-20
steps.description: Form I-20 (Certificate of Eligibility for Nonimmigrant Student Status) is issued by your school's DSO and is required for the F-1 visa application. Review every detail carefully.
steps.info: The I-20 is issued and maintained in SEVIS (Student and Exchange Visitor Information System). Your I-20 includes a SEVIS ID number (beginning with "N") that you will need throughout the process.
actions: Review the I-20 for accuracy: your full legal name (must match passport), date of birth, school, program, program start date, and program end date
Confirm the SEVIS ID number (starts with "N") — record this number securely
Confirm the issued I-20 lists the correct school and level of study
If any information is incorrect, contact your DSO immediately for a correction before proceeding
Review the I-20 student copy signature page — the DSO must have signed it
Store the original I-20 safely — you will need it throughout your F-1 status
steps.output: Form I-20 received, all details verified as correct, SEVIS ID recorded

steps.name: Pay the SEVIS I-901 Fee
steps.description: Before applying for an F-1 visa, you must pay the SEVIS I-901 fee at fmjfee.com. This fee supports the SEVIS database. Payment confirmation is required for the visa application.
steps.warn: Pay the SEVIS fee using the SEVIS ID (beginning with "N") from your I-20. The current fee amount is listed at fmjfee.com — verify before paying. Fees are subject to change.
actions: Visit fmjfee.com to pay the SEVIS I-901 fee
Enter your SEVIS ID (from your I-20) and personal information exactly as they appear on your I-20
Verify the current SEVIS fee amount at fmjfee.com before paying
Complete payment using an accepted method (credit/debit card or Western Union Quick Pay)
Save and print the SEVIS fee payment receipt (Form I-797 receipt confirmation)
Bring the payment receipt to your visa interview
steps.output: SEVIS I-901 fee paid; payment receipt saved

steps.name: Gather Visa Application Documents
steps.description: Before completing the DS-160 online application, gather all documents you will need. Having accurate information ready reduces errors and prevents having to restart the DS-160.
actions: Valid Pakistani passport (must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your intended period of stay)
Form I-20 (issued by your DSO)
SEVIS I-901 fee payment receipt
School acceptance letter
Financial evidence (bank statements, scholarship letters, sponsor letters)
Academic transcripts and diplomas
Standardized test score reports
Passport-style photograph meeting U.S. visa photo requirements
Any prior U.S. visa documentation (if applicable)
steps.output: All required documents gathered and organized

steps.name: Complete Form DS-160 (Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application)
steps.description: Form DS-160 is the online nonimmigrant visa application submitted at the Consular Electronic Application Center (CEAC). Complete it carefully and accurately — it is reviewed by the consular officer.
actions: Go to ceac.state.gov and start a new DS-160 application
Select the correct U.S. embassy or consulate in Pakistan where you will apply (Islamabad, Karachi, or Lahore)
Complete all required fields accurately and consistently with your other documents
Upload a compliant passport-style photograph meeting the specifications at travel.state.gov/photos
Review all answers carefully before submitting — mistakes can cause delays
Submit the DS-160 and print the confirmation page (barcode page) — you will need this at your interview
Save your DS-160 application ID in case you need to retrieve or update your application
steps.output: DS-160 submitted; confirmation barcode page printed and saved

steps.name: Pay the MRV Visa Application Fee
steps.description: The Machine Readable Visa (MRV) fee is required to schedule a visa interview appointment. This fee is non-refundable. Pay through the official U.S. visa appointment portal for Pakistan (CGI Federal at ustraveldocs.com/pk).
steps.warn: Verify the current MRV fee at ustraveldocs.com/pk before paying — fee amounts can change. Do not pay through third-party sites.
actions: Go to ustraveldocs.com/pk and create an account or log in
Select Pakistan and the visa category (Student – F)
Verify the current MRV fee amount and pay using an accepted payment method
Save the MRV fee payment receipt and transaction number — you will need it to schedule your appointment
Note: MRV fees are generally non-refundable and non-transferable; confirm the policy on the payment portal
steps.output: MRV visa application fee paid; payment receipt and transaction number saved

steps.name: Schedule Visa Interview Appointment
steps.description: After paying the MRV fee, schedule your visa interview appointment through ustraveldocs.com/pk. U.S. visa interviews for Pakistan are conducted at the U.S. Embassy Islamabad, U.S. Consulate General Karachi, and U.S. Consulate General Lahore.
steps.info: Appointment wait times vary. Check current wait times at travel.state.gov/waitingtimes or ustraveldocs.com/pk. Apply well in advance of your I-20 program start date — SEVP recommends applying for the F-1 visa no more than 120 days before your I-20 start date.
actions: Log in to ustraveldocs.com/pk using your profile and MRV fee receipt number
Select your preferred consular post (Islamabad, Karachi, or Lahore)
Select an available appointment date and time
Confirm the appointment and save or print the appointment confirmation
Check the current appointment wait time at travel.state.gov/waitingtimes
Note: you may apply for your F-1 visa up to 120 days before your I-20 program start date — do not schedule earlier than this window per SEVP guidance
steps.output: Visa interview appointment scheduled; appointment confirmation saved

steps.name: Prepare Interview Documents
steps.description: Assemble all documents required for the visa interview. Review the U.S. Embassy Pakistan student visa page for any locally required additions or specific instructions.
actions: Valid Pakistani passport
DS-160 confirmation/barcode page (printed)
MRV fee payment receipt
Visa appointment confirmation letter
Form I-20 (original, signed by DSO)
SEVIS I-901 fee payment receipt
School acceptance letter
Financial documentation
Academic transcripts and diplomas/certificates
Standardized and English proficiency test score reports
One photograph meeting U.S. visa photo requirements
steps.output: Complete interview document package assembled and organized

steps.name: Attend the Visa Interview
steps.description: Appear at the designated U.S. Embassy or Consulate in Pakistan for your interview. Answer all questions from the consular officer honestly and clearly. The officer will primarily assess your nonimmigrant intent and eligibility.
steps.info: F-1 applicants must demonstrate nonimmigrant intent — that you intend to study and return home after completing your program. The consular officer may ask about your program of study, funding, ties to Pakistan, and plans after graduation. Answer confidently and truthfully.
actions: Arrive at the consulate location at the time specified
Bring all assembled documents in an organized manner
Do not bring prohibited items (check the consulate's prohibited items list)
Answer the consular officer's questions about your study plans, school, funding, and ties to Pakistan honestly and clearly
If approved: note whether the passport will be returned by mail/courier or for in-person pickup
If placed in administrative processing (221(g)): follow the instructions and provide any additional requested documents promptly
steps.output: Visa interview completed; outcome noted (approved / pending / additional processing)

steps.name: Receive Visa-Stamped Passport
steps.description: If approved, the consulate affixes the F-1 visa stamp to your passport. In Pakistan, approved passports are returned by courier/mail or designated pickup. Follow the specific instructions given at interview.
actions: Track your passport return through the tracking system
Upon receipt, verify the visa stamp: name, date of birth, visa type (F-1), number of entries, and visa expiration date
Confirm the visa start date — you may NOT enter the U.S. more than 30 days before your I-20 program start date on an F-1 visa
Report any errors on the visa to the consulate immediately
Store your passport and I-20 together safely for travel
steps.output: F-1 visa stamp received and verified in passport

steps.name: Confirm I-20 Program Start Date & Entry Window
steps.description: F-1 students may enter the United States no more than 30 days before the program start date listed on their I-20. Entering earlier than this window is not permitted on an F-1 visa.
steps.warn: Do not book travel to arrive more than 30 days before your I-20 program start date. This is a legal requirement under F-1 rules.
actions: Confirm the program start date on your I-20
Calculate the earliest permitted U.S. arrival date (30 days before the I-20 start date)
Book travel to arrive within the permitted 30-day window
Contact your DSO if you have any questions about your entry window or if you need a deferred start date
steps.output: Entry window confirmed; travel booked within the permitted 30-day window

steps.name: Book Travel and Accommodations
steps.description: Book your flight to the U.S. and confirm housing arrangements. Notify your school's International Student Office of your expected arrival date.
actions: Book an international flight arriving within your permitted F-1 entry window
Confirm housing: on-campus dormitory or off-campus arrangement
Notify the International Student Office / DSO of your expected arrival date
Research orientation program dates
Ensure your carry-on luggage will include all essential travel and immigration documents
steps.output: Flight booked; housing confirmed; DSO notified of arrival date

steps.name: Organize Travel Documents
steps.description: Carry all required immigration documents in your carry-on baggage. These documents are essential for CBP inspection upon arrival in the United States.
actions: Valid Pakistani passport with F-1 visa stamp
Original Form I-20 (signed by DSO) — carry in carry-on
SEVIS I-901 fee payment receipt
School acceptance letter
Financial documentation
DS-160 confirmation page (optional but recommended)
Contact information for your school's DSO and U.S. address
Keep all documents together in an organized folder in your carry-on
steps.output: All required travel documents organized in carry-on baggage

steps.name: Attend Pre-Departure Orientation (if available)
steps.description: Many Pakistani universities and organizations (e.g., U.S. Embassy Pakistan, Fulbright program, EducationUSA) offer pre-departure orientations for students traveling to the U.S.
actions: Check if your school or the U.S. Embassy Pakistan offers a pre-departure orientation
Check EducationUSA Pakistan at educationusa.state.gov for orientation resources
Review Study in the States pre-departure guidance
Research the city and state where your school is located
steps.output: Pre-departure orientation attended or study materials reviewed

steps.name: Clear U.S. Customs and Border Protection
steps.description: Upon arriving at a U.S. port of entry, you will go through CBP inspection. Present your documents to the CBP officer.
steps.info: CBP officers may ask about your plans, school, and length of stay. Answer honestly and consistently. Keep your I-20 and passport accessible.
actions: Present your valid passport with F-1 visa stamp to the CBP officer
Present your original Form I-20
Complete any required CBP arrival forms
Answer the CBP officer's questions about your program, school, and stay honestly
CBP will stamp your passport and note your admission as "F-1 D/S" (Duration of Status)
Confirm your admission stamp shows "F-1" and "D/S"
steps.output: Admitted to the U.S. as F-1 student; passport stamped with F-1 D/S

steps.name: Retrieve Your I-94 Arrival Record
steps.description: CBP automatically creates an electronic I-94 arrival/departure record for most air and sea arrivals.
actions: Visit i94.cbp.dhs.gov within a few days of arrival to retrieve your electronic I-94
Verify the I-94 shows: your full name, date of birth, class of admission "F-1", and admit-until date "D/S"
Save and print a copy of your I-94 for your records
If there is any error on the I-94, contact CBP promptly to correct it
steps.output: I-94 retrieved; confirms F-1 admission and D/S

steps.name: Report to Your School & Check In with DSO
steps.description: F-1 regulations require you to report to your school by the report date on your I-20. Your DSO must update your SEVIS record to show you have arrived and are enrolled.
steps.warn: Failure to report to your school and have your DSO update SEVIS can cause your SEVIS record to be terminated.
actions: Arrive at your school campus by or before the I-20 report date
Check in with the International Student Office and present your passport, visa, I-20, and I-94
Your DSO will update your SEVIS record to confirm your arrival and enrollment
Confirm your class registration and review any enrollment requirements
Attend any mandatory orientation sessions
steps.output: Reported to school; DSO has updated SEVIS record; enrollment confirmed

steps.name: Understand Full-Time Enrollment Requirements
steps.description: F-1 students must maintain a full course load each semester. Dropping below full-time without prior DSO authorization can jeopardize your F-1 status.
actions: Confirm your school's definition of full-time enrollment for F-1 purposes
Review Study in the States guidance on enrollment requirements
If you need to drop below full-time for medical or academic reasons: consult your DSO before making changes
Do not withdraw from courses without first consulting your DSO
Register for each semester on time to maintain continuous enrollment
steps.output: Full-time enrollment maintained; any reduced course load properly authorized by DSO

steps.name: Keep Your I-20 and Documents Current
steps.description: Your I-20 must accurately reflect your current program, school, and expected graduation date. Any changes require a new or updated I-20 from your DSO.
actions: Review your I-20 program end date regularly
If you transfer to a new school: follow the official SEVIS transfer process through your DSO
If you change your major or level of study: obtain an updated I-20 from your DSO
Ensure your U.S. residential address is always current with your DSO
Your passport must remain valid — renew it at the Pakistani Embassy/Consulate in the U.S. well before it expires
steps.output: I-20 current and accurate; address updated with DSO; passport valid

steps.name: Understand On-Campus and Off-Campus Employment Rules
steps.description: F-1 students have limited employment authorization. On-campus employment of up to 20 hours per week during school sessions is generally permitted without additional authorization.
steps.warn: Working without authorization is a serious F-1 violation that can result in status termination. Always consult your DSO before accepting any employment.
actions: Review the on-campus employment rules with your DSO
Do not engage in any off-campus employment without prior DSO authorization and, where required, USCIS approval
If you need work authorization: ask your DSO about Curricular Practical Training (CPT) or Optional Practical Training (OPT)
Review USCIS CPT guidance and OPT guidance
Keep copies of any employment authorization documents
steps.output: Employment rules understood; any employment is properly authorized

steps.name: Travel Outside the U.S. (Re-entry Preparation)
steps.description: F-1 students may travel outside the U.S. and return, provided their visa is still valid, their I-20 is current, and they have a valid DSO travel signature on their I-20.
actions: Ensure your F-1 visa stamp is still valid for re-entry before traveling
Obtain a travel signature from your DSO on your I-20 before departure
Carry your valid passport, valid I-20 with travel signature, and any OPT EAD card when re-entering
If your visa has expired while in the U.S. and you travel outside: you must apply for a new F-1 visa at a U.S. consulate before returning
Review Study in the States guidance on travel
steps.output: Travel signature obtained from DSO; valid visa confirmed; re-entry prepared

steps.name: Explore OPT (Optional Practical Training)
steps.description: F-1 students may apply for OPT — temporary employment authorization directly related to their major area of study.
steps.info: OPT applications are filed with USCIS and must be submitted through your DSO. Apply well in advance — USCIS processing can take several months.
actions: Consult your DSO about OPT eligibility and timing
Determine if you want pre-completion or post-completion OPT
Request an OPT I-20 recommendation from your DSO
File Form I-765 (Application for Employment Authorization) with USCIS
Verify the current USCIS filing fee for Form I-765
Do not begin OPT employment until you have received your Employment Authorization Document (EAD)
If you have a qualifying STEM degree: ask your DSO about the STEM OPT extension
steps.output: OPT application strategy understood; application filed or deferred based on timeline

steps.name: Program Completion and Grace Period
steps.description: After completing your F-1 program (or OPT), you have a 60-day grace period during which you may prepare to depart, change visa status, or transfer to another school. You may not work during this grace period unless otherwise authorized.
actions: Confirm your program completion date (listed on I-20)
After program completion: you have a 60-day grace period — use it to prepare for departure, apply for a change of status, or transfer to another school program
During the 60-day grace period you may not work
If you intend to remain in the U.S. after the grace period: consult an immigration attorney or your DSO about a change of status
If departing the U.S.: ensure you depart within the 60-day grace period
Review Study in the States guidance for current program completion rules
steps.output: Program completion timeline known; post-completion plans confirmed
